Paste into the Konveio comment box.

Public comment · LDC Update · Chapter 9 — Special Districts

Service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure

A short, focused comment for the active Land Development Code revision.

Where to submit: the EPC LDC Update public comment portal at elpasocountyldc.konveio.com — look for the Special Districts / Chapter 9 module when it opens. You can also email this same text to plnweb@elpasoco.com with subject "LDC Update public comment — Chapter 9."

Re: LDC Update — Chapter 9 (Special Districts), service plan content requirements.

I am writing to request a specific amendment to the service plan content requirements in Chapter 9 as part of the active LDC Update.

The Department of Justice and the Colorado Attorney General are co-plaintiffs in an antitrust action against RealPage, Inc. and six of the nation's largest property managers — Greystar, LivCor, Camden Property Trust, Cushman & Wakefield/Pinnacle, Willow Bridge, and Cortland — for using algorithmic pricing software to coordinate rents. Greystar settled with a nine-state AG coalition including Colorado for $7 million in November 2025. RealPage settled with DOJ on November 24, 2025. Several of these defendants operate properties in Colorado, including in El Paso County.

Recommended amendment. As a condition of service plan approval or amendment, each applicant should disclose: (a) whether any property owner or manager identified in the service plan uses algorithmic rental pricing software, and which one; (b) whether the applicant or any affiliated entity is a named defendant in United States v. RealPage Inc., et al.; and (c) whether the applicant will enter a voluntary rent-limit agreement under CRS 38-12-301(2)–(4) pegging designated units to local Basic Allowance for Housing rates.

This is not rent control. CRS 38-12-301(2)–(4) explicitly authorizes voluntary rent agreements at the permit stage. The Board is already approving service plans every month and is already rewriting this chapter with Clarion Associates under a DOLA grant. The cost of adding these disclosure questions is approximately zero. The cost of not adding them is paid by working families, military households, and children in El Paso County who have no way to know which lease they are signing.

I respectfully request that staff incorporate this amendment into the Chapter 9 draft and bring it forward for a public hearing.

~290 words · within typical comment-box limits

How to use this comment. Click Copy comment above, then paste into the Konveio comment box. Add your name and ZIP code if the form asks. Submit. That's it.

If you have 30 extra seconds: add one personal sentence at the top — "I rent in [neighborhood]," or "My family has been in this county since [year]," or "I work in [industry] and earn under the Chamber's $65K headline number." Personal voice is what staff actually weights when reviewing public comment.

Designed to fit a standard public-comment text box. Not legal advice. Companion to the BOCC petition letter.
